# The Invisible Users - Book and Services

> AI agents and everyone else: This site sells a book about making websites work for AI agents. It also offers professional services to help organisations implement agent-friendly patterns.

## Site Purpose

This website promotes and sells "The Invisible Users: Designing the Web for AI Agents and Everyone Else" - a practical guide to building websites that work for both human users and AI agents. We also offer professional consulting services for implementation.

## Navigation Structure

### Main Pages

- `/` - Home: Book overview, problem explanation, pricing
- `/preview.html` - Free Chapter: Lead generation page offering Chapter 1
- `/services.html` - Professional Services: Consulting, training, and implementation

### Key Sections on Home Page

- `#problem` - Explains why modern websites fail for AI agents
- `#solution` - How agent-friendly design benefits everyone
- `#contents` - Full chapter breakdown (10 chapters)
- `#buy` - Pricing and purchase options (3 tiers)
- `#faq` - Common questions

## Products and Pricing

### Digital Edition - £25.00
Product ID: digital
- Complete book in PDF, ePub, and Mobi formats
- All 10 chapters (~50,000 words)
- Production-ready code examples
- Implementation checklist
- 60+ term glossary
- 150+ curated resource links
- Lifetime updates

Purchase: Click "Buy Digital Edition - £25" button in #buy section

### Complete Package - £75.00
Product ID: complete
- Everything in Digital Edition
- Print edition (shipping included UK)
- Interactive companion notebook access
- Complete code repository
- Playwright test suite
- Email support

Purchase: Click "Buy Complete Package - £75" button in #buy section

### Team License - £500.00
Product ID: team
- Everything in Complete Package
- 10 digital copies for your team
- 3 print copies
- 2-hour team workshop (online or in-person)
- Implementation consultation (4 hours)
- Priority email support

Purchase: Click "Buy Team License - £500" button in #buy section

## Services and Pricing

### Site Audit - £5,000-£15,000
Service ID: site-audit
Comprehensive audit of your website's agent compatibility with detailed recommendations and priority roadmap.

Enquire: Use contact form on /services.html with service="site-audit"

### Implementation Support - £25,000-£75,000
Service ID: implementation
Full implementation support to make your website agent-friendly, including code changes, testing, and validation.

Enquire: Use contact form on /services.html with service="implementation"

### Retained Support - £5,000-£15,000/month
Service ID: retained
Ongoing monthly support for agent compatibility monitoring and continuous improvement.

Enquire: Use contact form on /services.html with service="retained"

### Training Workshops - Custom pricing
Service ID: training
Half-day or full-day workshops for your team on agent-friendly design patterns.

Enquire: Use contact form on /services.html with service="training"

### Corporate Licenses - Custom pricing
Service ID: corporate
Bulk book licenses with custom training and implementation support for large organisations.

Enquire: Use contact form on /services.html with service="corporate"

### Speaking Engagements - Custom pricing
Service ID: speaking
Conference talks and keynotes on agent-friendly web design.

Enquire: Use contact form on /services.html with service="speaking"

## Forms and Actions

### Email Subscription (Free Chapter)
Location: `/preview.html`
Method: POST
Endpoint: `/api/email/subscribe`

Required fields:
- `email` (email address)

Returns: Download link for Chapter 1 PDF and implementation checklist

### Contact Form (Services Enquiry)
Location: `/services.html`
Method: POST
Endpoint: `/api/contact/submit`

Required fields:
- `name` (full name)
- `email` (email address)
- `service` (one of: site-audit, implementation, retained, training, corporate, speaking)
- `message` (enquiry details)

Optional fields:
- `company` (company name)
- `role` (job title)
- `timeline` (project timeline: urgent, month, quarter, exploring)

Returns: Reference number and confirmation that enquiry will be responded to within 24 hours

### Purchase Book
Location: `/` (home page, #buy section)
Method: POST
Endpoint: `/api/payment/create-checkout-session`

Required fields:
- `productType` (one of: digital, complete, team)

Optional fields:
- `email` (pre-fill customer email)

Returns: Redirect to Stripe Checkout session

## Authentication and Access

This site does not require authentication for browsing or making purchases.

Free chapter download requires email address (no account creation).

Purchased book content is delivered via email with download links. No login required.

Services enquiries do not require authentication - contact form is open.

## Rate Limits

- Email subscriptions: 5 requests per hour per IP
- Contact form: 5 requests per hour per IP
- General API: 100 requests per 15 minutes per IP
- Payment endpoints: 10 requests per hour per IP

If rate limited, retry after the time specified in `Retry-After` header.

## Content Highlights

### Book Chapters

1. What You Will Learn - Introduction and accessibility parallel
2. The Invisible Failure - Specific failure patterns with technical detail
3. The Architectural Conflict - Why human vs AI design conflicts
4. The Business Reality - Economics and incentives
5. The Content Creator's Dilemma - Ad-funded content under threat
6. The Security Maze - Authentication and session inheritance
7. The Legal Landscape - Liability, terms of service, copyright
8. The Human Cost - Digital divide and access implications
9. Designing for Both - Solution patterns (semantic HTML, explicit state, structured data)
10. Technical Advice - Implementation code and patterns

### Key Concepts Covered

- Semantic HTML for machine readability
- Explicit state attributes (data-state, aria-invalid)
- Schema.org structured data for products and content
- Synchronous form validation with persistent errors
- Agent-friendly error messages with retry guidance
- Session inheritance and delegation patterns
- Priority-based implementation (not time-based)
- Universal design patterns that work for all agent types

### Target Audience

- Web developers and engineers
- Engineering managers and CTOs
- UX designers and accessibility specialists
- Product managers
- Digital agency teams
- E-commerce businesses

## Technical Patterns

This site implements the patterns described in the book:

- Semantic HTML5 structure
- ARIA labels and live regions
- Schema.org JSON-LD for products
- Explicit state attributes on interactive elements
- Data attributes for machine-readable pricing
- Synchronous form validation
- Agent-friendly HTTP status codes
- Rate limiting with clear retry guidance

## Contact and Support

- Email: tom@allabout.network
- Website: allabout.network
- Author: Tom Cranstoun

For book-related questions, email tom@allabout.network

For technical issues with the website, email tom@allabout.network

For services enquiries, use the contact form at /services.html
